outlook_paragraph: Continental Resources (CTRA) shows reduced drilling activity heading into Q2 2023, with only 14 active sites detected via SAR and no newly active pads, resulting in a sharp negative relative activity delta of -0.8. This suggests a pullback in operational momentum, likely contributing to constrained production growth. With WTI averaging around $70.26 and a realized price differential of 78%, revenue is further moderated by only 40% exposure to the high-activity segment captured in the satellite data. The deterministic revenue forecast for the quarter ending June 2023 is $1.264B, reflecting these combined headwinds.
